data.content:
I can see your project — and the `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` test will fail since `"express"` returns `10.00m` instead of the expected `15.00m`.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. They want to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload to iterate on failing tests. I can see one test will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m for "express".

Let me set up `dotnet watch test` for them. I'll start it as an async process so they can see the output and iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4127c3e734a441b8b8acfc8a9a55bc22 && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test with hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content: `dotnet watch` is running! There's a build error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix it:
data.reasoningText: There's a build error — `ArgumentException` needs `using System;`. Let me fix that first, and `dotnet watch` will automatically pick up the change.
